question: What was the original scope of Regulation 22 in the SEBI (Investment Advisers) R
ref_answer: The original Regulation 22 (2013) was titled 'Segregation of execution services' and applied only to investment advisers that are banks, NBFCs, and body corporates which also provide distribution or execution services. Such entities could continue offering distribution services alongside advice provided they maintained operational segregation, ensured clients were not obligated to avail distribution services, maintained arms-length relationships, and ensured fees were paid directly to service providers. Individual investment advisers were not explicitly covered. The SEBI (Investment Advisers) (Amendment) Regulations, 2020 (w.e.f. 30-09-2020) completely substituted Regulation 22 — retitling it 'Client level segregation of advisory and distribution activities.' question: Describe the evolution of the certificate validity regime for credit rating agen
ref_answer: The evolution of certificate validity for CRAs under SEBI (CRA) Regulations, 1999 went through three stages: (1) Original (1999): The original regulation prescribed a fixed validity period of three years for the certificate of registration. (3) 2016 Amendment (w.e.f. 08-12-2016): The SEBI (Change in Conditions of Registration of Certain Intermediaries) (Amendment) Regulations, 2016 abolished both the initial/permanent registration distinction and all fixed validity periods — the certificate is now valid in perpetuity unless suspended or cancelled by SEBI.
